content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Thorough review of the graceful backend degradation feature. The implementation is well-structured and correct across all key areas: BackendExhausted failure class in domain.rs, pattern detection in process_backend.rs, terminal retry policy, panel degradation in final_review/completion/prompt_review, OpenRouter integration, probe-time filtering in the engine, and resume-path handling. Tests cover the key scenarios (detection patterns, non-retryability, one-exhausted degradation, all-exhausted failure). No critical bugs found. Two minor observations noted as amendments.

## Amendments

1. In final_review.rs, the post-loop quorum checks (line ~541 for proposals, line ~1150 for votes) return InsufficientPanelMembers without propagating a BackendExhausted failure class, unlike the equivalent checks in prompt_review.rs (line ~292) and completion.rs (line ~267) which use a last_exhaustion_error pattern. In practice the early-exit within the loop always catches the all-exhausted case, so this is not currently reachable as a bug. However, for defensive consistency, final_review.rs should adopt the same last_exhaustion_error propagation pattern so that any future refactoring that changes the early-exit logic doesn't silently degrade the failure classification.
   Rationale: If the early-exit logic in the proposal or vote loop is ever refactored (e.g. to batch invocations), the post-loop quorum check could become the primary failure path. Without last_exhaustion_error propagation, the engine would see no failure_class (None) and call fail_run_result — a harder failure mode than the intended non-retryable BackendExhausted. Matching the prompt_review/completion pattern is a low-cost defensive improvement.
2. In openrouter_backend.rs check_availability (line ~195), the probe targets /models/<model_id> which is a model metadata endpoint. Quota/billing exhaustion errors typically surface on /chat/completions, not /models. The is_backend_exhausted check on the probe response body may therefore never fire for real quota exhaustion. Consider either (a) documenting this as a known limitation in a code comment, or (b) adding a lightweight completions probe (e.g. a minimal request with max_tokens=1) as an alternative availability check when the models endpoint succeeds but the account may be quota-limited.
   Rationale: The runtime invocation path in map_http_error correctly detects exhaustion, so this is not a correctness gap — it only affects the pre-invocation probe optimization. Documenting the limitation prevents future developers from assuming the probe catches quota issues.
